critical.mandate: Preserve existing content not being changed — no silent drops, reorders, or modifications
Add version note documenting what changed and why
Update "Review Findings Incorporated" section after adversarial review

step.title: Load Existing UX Design
step.action: Read {planning_artifacts}/ux-design.md in full
Identify existing sections: personas, information architecture, wireframes, interaction patterns, accessibility
Identify existing Version History entries — note last version for auto-increment
Display current structure summary to user: section headers, persona count, wireframe count, current version

step.title: Identify Changes
step.ask: Is this linked to a change request? If so, provide the CR ID.
step.action: Classify change scope: MINOR (section update, text change) / SIGNIFICANT (new persona, new flow, navigation restructure) / BREAKING (complete redesign of major section)
Confirm scope of changes before proceeding

step.title: Apply Edits
step.action: For each affected section: present current content, propose edits, wait for user confirmation or modification
Preserve all unchanged sections exactly as-is — no reordering, no reformatting, no content loss
Validate consistency between edited sections and remaining unchanged sections
If edits affect FR-to-Screen Mapping: verify traceability remains accurate

step.title: Add Version Note
step.action: Append a new row to the Version History table: | {date} | {change summary} | {driver} | {CR ID or reference} |

If no Version History section exists, create one: ## Version History | Date | Change | Reason | CR/Reference | |------|--------|--------|-------------| | {date} | {change summary} | {driver} | {CR ID or reference} |

step.title: Save Updated UX Design
step.action: Generate a diff summary showing exactly what changed
step.template-output: Save updated UX design document with all edits applied, unchanged sections preserved, and version note added.

step.title: Adversarial Review
step.ask: The UX design has been updated. Would you like to run an adversarial review? This spawns a subagent in a separate context — recommended for significant changes. (yes / skip for minor edits)
step.action: If yes: spawn a subagent using the Agent tool: "Load {project-root}/_kratos/core/tasks/review-adversarial.xml. Read its entire contents. Target: {planning_artifacts}/ux-design.md. Target label: ux-design. Follow the task instructions EXACTLY."
If skip: proceed to step 7 — mark "Review Findings Incorporated" as "Incremental edit — adversarial review skipped (minor change)"
If ok: wait for user to confirm adversarial-review-ux-design-{date}.md exists

step.title: Incorporate Review Findings
step.action: Read {planning_artifacts}/adversarial-review-ux-design-{date}.md — extract critical and high severity findings
For each critical/high finding: incorporate into UX design document
Update the "Review Findings Incorporated" section — append new entries with amendment date
step.template-output: Update the UX design with adversarial review findings incorporated. Append to existing "Review Findings Incorporated" section with amendment date.

step.title: Cascade Impact Check
step.action: Read {planning_artifacts}/architecture.md section headers
Compare UX design changes against architecture and downstream artifacts
Classify cascade impact:
NONE: UX-only changes — architecture and stories unaffected
MINOR: architecture needs a section update — recommend /kratos-edit-arch
SIGNIFICANT: new components or interaction patterns affecting architecture — recommend /kratos-edit-arch with adversarial review, then /kratos-add-stories
Report cascade assessment to user with recommended next command(s)
